label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this question. The user provided a letter written to the next president and a question about the author's views on high school teachers. The question is asking which option correctly reflects the author's opinion when talking about high school teachers. First, I need to recall what the letter says about teachers. The relevant part of the letter is in the third paragraph. Let me read that again: "My final issue I would like to call to your attention is teacher's salaries. The high school students of today are America's future. In a way, America's future depends greatly on the high school teachers of America. Saying this, it is sad that teacher's salaries are where they are. While teachers are working harder and harder every day to teach high school students, CEO's of big companies that are driving our countries economy into the ground are getting bonuses. I'm not saying that teacher's salaries should be the same of CEO's of companies, but I do strongly believe that action should be taken to increase the salaries of teachers because they have one of the most important jobs in America - - preparing our country for the future." So the author is saying that teacher salaries are not high enough ("it is sad that teacher's salaries are where they are") and that they should be increased. The author also contrasts teachers with CEOs, noting that CEOs are getting bonuses even as teachers work hard, but the author clarifies
